title: Taiwan MND Detects 15 PLA Aircraft Sorties Crossing 4 ADIZ Sectors — Significant Spike
detail: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense detected 15 sorties of PLA aircraft and 7 PLAN vessels plus 2 official ships operating around Taiwan on March 28. A total of 11 of 15 sorties crossed the Taiwan Strait median line, entering Taiwan's northern, central, southwestern, and eastern ADIZ sectors simultaneously — the first time in March 2026 all four sectors were penetrated in a single day. Aircraft types confirmed: J-10 and J-16 fighters and a KJ-500 airborne early warning and control aircraft. PLA Eastern Theater Command described the activity as 'air-sea joint training.' The multi-sector crossing represents a significant spike from March 27's 6 sorties (4 crossing) and compares to the March 25 spike of 16 sorties (13 crossing). ROC Armed Forces deployed CAP aircraft, Navy ships, and coastal missile systems in response. Analysts note the uptick may reflect pre-summit signaling ahead of the rescheduled Trump-Xi meeting (now May 14–15), with PLA maintaining pressure on Taiwan's defense posture without crossing into exercise-level activity.

title: Two PLA Type 055 Destroyers Complete First Live-Fire Drill in East China Sea — Eastern Theater Debut
detail: China's PLA Navy newly commissioned Type 055 guided-missile destroyers Dongguan (Hull 109) and Anqing (Hull 110) — each displacing 13,200 tons — conducted their inaugural joint live-fire drill in the East China Sea in late March 2026. This marks the first Type 055 assignment specifically to the PLA Eastern Theater Command, which oversees Taiwan contingency operations. The exercise simulated coordinated strikes against air, surface, and subsurface threats. The Type 055 destroyer (NATO: Renhai-class) is China's most capable surface combatant, equipped with 112 vertical launch cells, advanced phased-array radar, and anti-ship/land-attack cruise missiles. Deploying two to the Eastern Theater adds significant strike capacity directly relevant to Taiwan and East China Sea contingencies. The drill adds to an already elevated Eastern Theater posture that includes increased ADIZ incursions and the March 25 aerial spike.
